purpose: Archive execution substrate taxonomy and carrier assumptions from the OPML without promoting them into workflow contracts by default.

substrate_classes.title: Multi-Account Control Agent
substrate_classes.why_archived: Account-surface taxonomy is a substrate concern, not a workflow-stage or orchestration contract.

substrate_classes.title: Single-Account Control Agent
source_signals: 只有控制单社交账号的agent
substrate_classes.why_archived: This distinction matters only if executor selection or risk policy really depends on account surface count.

substrate_classes.title: Accountless Persistent Agent
substrate_classes.why_archived: Continuity policy belongs to execution substrate design unless a downstream consumer explicitly needs the distinction.

substrate_classes.title: Accountless Limited-Memory Agent
substrate_classes.why_archived: Memory-limit taxonomy should not be fused into workflow shell or branch topology.

substrate_classes.title: Temporary External Helper
substrate_classes.why_archived: Temporary helper identity is a substrate/persona distinction, not a default execution-unit contract.

substrate_classes.title: Openclaw Execution Carrier
substrate_classes.why_archived: Concrete execution carrier choice is an environment assumption rather than a workflow contract by default.

default_rules: Keep substrate taxonomy separate from orchestration contracts unless executor selection really depends on it.
Do not treat account-bearing or accountless distinctions as branch topology.
Do not treat concrete carriers such as openclaw as workflow-state or branch-class semantics.
